summary: WebSocket real-time notifications testing completed for Volt Taxi app. Backend APIs working perfectly, WebSocket code implemented correctly, but external WebSocket connections failing due to routing issues.

critical_bugs.issue: WebSocket endpoints not accessible through external URL - connection timeout after 5 seconds
critical_bugs.impact: Prevents real-time notifications for ride updates, driver-passenger communication, and live connection status
critical_bugs.symptoms: WebSocket connections timeout, UI shows 'Hors ligne' (offline) status permanently
critical_bugs.root_cause: Kubernetes ingress/proxy configuration not routing WebSocket connections properly

integration_issues.issue: WebSocket connection status permanently shows 'Hors ligne' (offline) in UI header
integration_issues.symptoms: Connection indicator never changes from red 'Hors ligne' to green 'Live' status

passed_tests: Ride creation, acceptance, and completion workflow
WebSocket message types (new_ride, ride_accepted, ride_started, ride_completed)
action_item_for_main_agent: CRITICAL: Fix WebSocket routing configuration. WebSocket endpoints (/ws/driver/{token} and /ws/passenger/{token}) are not accessible through external URL. This breaks real-time notifications which are core to the taxi app functionality. Requires Kubernetes ingress configuration update to support WebSocket connections or alternative WebSocket server setup.

success_percentage.backend: 94% (15/16 API tests passed, WebSocket routing fails)
success_percentage.frontend: 85% (UI working, WebSocket integration blocked by backend routing)
